error.signature: UART framing error: start/stop bit mismatch

verdict.summary: UART produces garbage or framing errors. Almost always baud rate mismatch or parity mismatch.

dead_ends.action: Swap TX and RX wires assuming they are crossed
dead_ends.why_fails: TX/RX swap produces silence, not framing errors. Framing errors mean data arrives but cannot be decoded.

dead_ends.action: Add pull-up resistors to TX/RX lines
dead_ends.why_fails: UART is push-pull, not open-drain. Pull-ups do not fix framing errors.

dead_ends.action: Increase baud rate for faster communication
dead_ends.why_fails: The issue is mismatch, not speed. Both sides must agree on identical settings.

workarounds.action: Verify both sides use identical settings: baud, data bits, stop bits, parity
workarounds.how: Common default: 115200 8N1 (115200 baud, 8 data bits, no parity, 1 stop bit)

workarounds.action: Check clock source accuracy - HSI can drift causing baud errors
workarounds.how: STM32 HSI is +/-1% at 25C but worse at extremes. Use HSE crystal for reliable UART.

workarounds.action: Use oscilloscope or logic analyzer to measure actual bit timing
workarounds.how: Measure shortest pulse; 1/width = actual baud rate
